title: Interpret Available Tote Management API Functions On The Aveva API Page
summary: Use the displayed Aveva API page titled "API Features for Tote Management" to identify the tote management functions shown and interpret each function only by the short description visible in the source.
when_to_use: Use when support personnel need to identify which tote management API functions are available on the displayed Aveva API page and understand the documented purpose of each listed function.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to execute API actions.
Do not use this runbook to infer parameters, access paths, execution steps, or outcomes beyond the short descriptions shown in the source.
Do not use this runbook if the displayed page does not show the tote management function list clearly enough to read.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Aveva API page, identify the listed tote management functions, and record each function name with its documented purpose exactly as shown.

safety_notes: This is a reference-only interpretation procedure derived from a training source.
Do not infer execution behavior, parameters, or controls beyond the visible descriptions.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Aveva API page or training video frame showing API Features for Tote Management
Ability to read the listed function names and descriptions

access_required: Access to the displayed Aveva API page or the referenced training video frame artifact
system_preconditions: The API Features for Tote Management page must be visible and readable

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the Aveva API page with the tote management feature list.

steps.title: View the tote management API page
steps.purpose: Establish the correct source page for interpretation.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Aveva API page that shows the heading "API Features for Tote Management."
steps.expected_result: The tote management API page is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The page heading and function list are visible and readable.
steps.failure_condition: The page is not visible, is the wrong page, or the text is not readable.

actions.label: View API page
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Aveva API page that shows the heading "API Features for Tote Management."

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Aveva API page showing the heading/API Features for Tote Management and the tote management feature list.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed page does not show the listed tote management functions.
The descriptions are not readable.

steps.title: Locate the listed tote management functions
steps.purpose: Identify the set of functions documented on the page.
steps.instruction: Locate the listed tote management functions on the page, including Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.
steps.expected_result: All listed tote management functions are identified on the page.
steps.healthy_condition: Each named function is visible in the feature list.
steps.failure_condition: One or more listed functions cannot be found or read on the displayed page.

actions.label: Identify function list
actions.exact_action: Locate the listed tote management functions on the page, including Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The visible function names: Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed page does not show the listed tote management functions.
The function names are not readable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR-visible items include Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.

steps.title: Match each function to its documented description
steps.purpose: Interpret the page content without adding unsupported behavior.
steps.instruction: Read the short description next to each function and match it to the documented purpose shown in the source.
steps.expected_result: Each listed function is paired with its visible documented purpose.
steps.healthy_condition: Function names and purposes are matched exactly as shown on the page or in the source summary.
steps.failure_condition: Descriptions are missing, unreadable, or require inference beyond the source.

actions.label: Read descriptions
actions.exact_action: Read the short description next to each function and match it to the documented purpose shown in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The short descriptions associated with each tote management function on the Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: The descriptions are not readable.
Interpreting the function would require inferring behavior beyond the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: "Add Tote" adds a specified tote to the system and "Remove Tote" removes a specified tote from the system.
"Get Tote Task" retrieves the current tote task; "Resend Tote Task" resends a command to a tote that encountered an error or failed to execute.
"Exchange Tote" manually triggers a tote exchange by tote ID, "Exchange Lane" by lane number, and "Get Exchange Settings" retrieves tote exchange settings and configurations.

steps.title: Verify Add Tote description
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented purpose of Add Tote from the source.
steps.instruction: For Add Tote, verify that the page describes it as adding a specified tote to the system.
steps.expected_result: Add Tote is interpreted exactly as documented.
steps.healthy_condition: Add Tote is recorded as adding a specified tote to the system.
steps.failure_condition: The Add Tote description is missing, unreadable, or interpreted beyond the source wording.

success_criteria: The user identifies the tote management API functions shown on the page.
Each function is matched to the documented purpose supported by the source.
No execution steps, parameters, or outcomes are inferred beyond the short descriptions shown.
healthy_conditions: The API Features for Tote Management page is visible and readable.
The listed functions can be identified on the page.
The documented purpose of each function can be captured exactly as shown.
failure_conditions: The displayed page does not show the listed tote management functions.
The function names or descriptions are not readable.
The task requires execution details, parameters, or outcomes not present in the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed page does not show the listed tote management functions or if the descriptions are not readable.
Escalate if a user requests execution steps, parameters, or behavior not supported by the source.
Do not infer execution steps, parameters, or outcomes beyond the short descriptions shown in the source.
